channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Does Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis Have Early Access?
item.content:encoded:
The next year of major titles has started to take shape, with Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis among the highlights, and early access details have already emerged.

The reimagining of the Tomb Raider story from Crystal Dynamics has been pushed back to a February 2027 release date, joining several high-profile releases in that window, including the likes of Fable.

Jumping in with early access has become a popular approach for gamers, and Insider Gaming has the details on whether this is possible in Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis.

Does Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis Have Early Access?

Yes, Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis has an early access period that enables players to jump in up to two days earlier than the standard release date.

Early access periods have become increasingly common in modern gaming, often resulting in what is essentially two release dates, one for those with early access and another for the standard release date.

  • More Insider Gaming: Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis Gets a New Trailer

However, early access is often locked behind an additional purchase, and that is once again the case for Legacy of Atlantis, as those who want to be among the first to play the game will have to fork out a bit more for the privilege.

How to Get Legacy of Atlantis Early Access

The early access window of Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis is only available for those who splash out for the Deluxe Edition or the Collector’s Edition of the title.

A 48-hour early access period is one of the main incentives for purchasing one of the more expensive editions of the latest Tomb Raider title, alongside outfits, DLC packs, and, in the case of the Collector’s Edition, a host of physical bonuses.

  • More Insider Gaming: Sony State of Play June 2026 Showcase—Everything Announced

That means that a minimum of an extra $10 will need to be paid in order to jump into Legacy of Atlantis during the early access period, which won’t be worth the outlay for many players.

It could end up being decent value for money, however, based on the price of the DLC Story Packs when they are released post-launch.

What were your impressions of the first Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis gameplay? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more from the June 2026 PlayStation State of Play, read confirmation Spider-Man won’t appear in Marvel’s Wolverine, and check out a returning feature to the God of War franchise.

item.title: Is Marvel’s Wolverine Open-World?
item.content:encoded:
The countdown for Marvel’s Wolverine is well and truly on, with the release date in sight, and details of the game’s approach and open-world options have been confirmed.

After establishing a Marvel universe with the Spider-Man series of games, all of which feature open-world mechanics, it’s understandable that fans are beginning to wonder whether Marvel’s Wolverine will include similar mechanics.

If you’re unsure about the open-world details of Marvel’s Wolverine, Insider Gaming has you covered.

Is Marvel’s Wolverine Open-World?

No, Marvel’s Wolverine will not be an open-world title, and will instead focus on a linear story path, visiting various locations.

This will make the Wolverine title a significantly different experience to Insomniac Games’ Spider-Man titles, which features vast open-world gameplay in New York, including a variety of side objectives, crime to stop, and collectibles to uncover.

  • More Insider Gaming: Spider-Man Won’t Appear in Marvel’s Wolverine

However, this makes sense for Spider-Man, who is, for the most part, a street-level hero, focusing on his role as the friendly neighbourhood Spider-Man and protecting New York.

Wolverine, meanwhile, is focused on protecting mutants spread worldwide, and with no indication that Charles Xavier has established the X-Men in the Insomniac Games universe, there’s no reason for Wolverine to be based in a specific location. Instead, he’ll travel the world.

That could, of course, change in the future if Insomniac Games wants to establish a base of operations for the X-Men, but having this approach initially makes a lot of sense.

What Type of Game is Marvel’s Wolverine?

Marvel’s Wolverine is a single-player, story-driven adventure that sees Logan travel to various locations worldwide, fighting to protect mutants.

Speaking to IGN, game director Mike Daly described Insomniac Games’ creation as having “comic book-style pacing, dense story, that’s like a rollercoaster, and it grips you from beginning to end.

  • More Insider Gaming: Marvel’s Wolverine Features Logan Teaming up With a Surprising Character

“I can say we did not set out to make an open-world game or a sandbox game. What we really wanted was high octane, high intrigue, linear single-player adventure, and the missions reflect that in their structure.”

While there will be collectibles and secrets to uncover in Marvel’s Wolverine, potentially leading you to replay the game to unlock everything, it won’t have the same sort of features as Spider-Man.

Are you happy with the lack of open-world in Marvel’s Wolverine? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more on Marvel’s Wolverine, read about the option to turn off gore in the title, and check out the pre-order details.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Insomniac Games Confirms Spider-Man Won’t Appear in Marvel’s Wolverine
item.content:encoded:
Attention towards Marvel’s Wolverine is continuing to rise ahead of launch later this year, with excitement levels fuelled further by the latest gameplay trailer, but Insomniac Games has moved early to avoid any confusion by confirming Spider-Man won’t appear in the title.

After three successful Spider-Man games to flesh out their comic book universe, bringing the likes of Peter Parker, Miles Morales, Venom, and plenty more to life, Insomniac Games is turning its focus away from New York to bring an adventure across the world, putting players in control of Wolverine.

However, while the debut of the X-Men icon does take place in the same universe as Spider-Man, it has already been confirmed that there will not be a cameo from the web-swinging fan-favorite.

No Spider-Man Cameo in Marvel’s Wolverine

In an interview with IGN, Marcus Smith, creative director of Marvel’s Wolverine, put to bed any hope of a Spider-Man cameo in the title, making it clear there will not be any such appearance.

“It’s correct that it does take place in the 1048 Marvel Universe, which is the Insomniac video game universe. It happens in the same world, but we don’t have any crossover. Spider-Man will not be making an appearance in Wolverine.”

While the news may be disappointing for anyone who hoped to see the two iconic Marvel characters meet, it does make sense from a story perspective, particularly as Insomniac has made it clear that the existence of mutants is not widely known in the universe.

Therefore, Spider-Man himself likely doesn’t know that mutants exist, and would therefore have no reason to cross paths with Wolverine in the upcoming title.

There is still a chance for Peter Parker and Logan to come face-to-face somewhere in the future, however, with Insomniac continuing to make Marvel titles to expand their universe.

When the existence of mutants comes to light, or if the X-Men get established in the Insomniac universe, the likelihood of Wolverine and Spider-Man crossing paths increases.

Spider-Man and Wolverine have teamed up on many occasions in the world of comic books, and are set to meet in the Marvel Cinematic Universe with Avengers: Doomsday and Secret Wars, but, for now, they will be kept apart in Insomniac Games’ world.

Are you disappointed there won’t be a Spider-Man cameo in Marvel’s Wolverine? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more on Marvel’s Wolverine, read about the option to turn off gore, and find out details of a surprising ally for Logan in the game.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Marvel’s Wolverine Features Logan Teaming up With a Surprising Character
item.content:encoded:
Excitement levels for Marvel’s Wolverine are rising following the latest gameplay footage revealed during Sony’s State of Play, and further details have revealed a team-up with a surprising character.

Trailers and gameplay footage have revealed a number of recognisable characters set to make an appearance in the title, with Jean Grey, Leech, Omega Red, Sentinels, and more all being spotted.

The latest gameplay trailer also revealed Sabertooth will be making an appearance, but it turns out he will actually be an ally for Logan in the game.

Logan Teams Up With Sabertooth in Marvel’s Wolverine

In an interview with IGN, game director Mike Daly revealed that Sabertooth will be teaming up with Logan in Marvel’s Wolverine, and will therefore not be one of the main villains.

“In this game, Logan and Sabretooth are actually on a team together,” Daly explained, “but the way they ally is not really anything like the way you ally with Jean Grey.”

“Logan and Sabretooth have a very competitive dynamic between them. Whereas Jean Grey might give you an opportunity to get a kill, with Sabretooth fighting alongside you, it’s more like, you think you’ve got a kill and he rushes in to steal it from you, and their combat banter brings out that dynamic and makes the whole fight more fun.”

It remains to be seen whether Logan and Sabertooth go head-to-head at any point in the story, but given the setting of Marvel’s Wolverine features mutants in hiding, and the majority of the world not knowing about their existence, it does make sense for their goals to be aligned.

That could, of course, change as Marvel’s Wolverine develops further in Insomniac Games’ universe, and having Sabertooth turn from friend to foe as the series develops could certainly add further layers to the story.

What do you think about Wolverine and Sabertooth being allies? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more on Marvel’s Wolverine, read about the option to turn off gore in the game, and check out the pre-order details.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Marvel’s Wolverine Includes Option to Turn Off Gore
item.content:encoded:
We’re into the final stretch in the wait for Insomniac Games’ latest expansion of their comic book universe with the launch of Marvel’s Wolverine, and additional details have been revealed, confirming that gore can be turned off.

Insomniac have been entrusted with another one of Marvel’s most iconic characters in Wolverine, after superb results with their Spider-Man series of games, but taking the reins with Wolverine is an entirely different prospect from the web slinger.

Trailers revealed so far have indicated a high level of gore, with brutal takedowns and dismemberment, but, for those looking for a lighter tone, it has been confirmed that gore can be turned off in the settings.

You Can Turn Off Gore in Marvel’s Wolverine

In an interview with IGN off the back of the latest gameplay trailer released at Sony’s State of Play, game director Mike Daly detailed the importance of violence when replicating Wolverine, but confirmed the option to turn off gore.

“When you look at the aspects of the character and all the stories he’s been through in his history, it was clear that violence was a key part of that,” Daly explained.

“Now, all that being said, we know that it’s not for everyone. And because we knew this was going to be a big part of the game from the beginning, we also implemented an accessibility feature to turn off gore.

“It’s clearly integrated into the game in a lot of different ways, and so it’s a nuanced feature that is very selective about what we show, what we censor, turning off blood and things like that, just to make it more palatable to people who don’t really want to have that part of the experience.”

It’s been known for a while that Marvel’s Wolverine features a higher age rating than the Spider-Man titles, with an M rating by the ESRB and an 18+ rating by PEGI, compared to the T rating by the ESRB and a PEGI 16 rating for Spider-Man 2.

Will you be turning off gore in Marvel’s Wolverine when it drops later this year? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Marvel’s Wolverine, check out the pre-order details for the game, and check out details from the latest action-packed gameplay trailer.

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Escape from Tarkov PvE Players Struck by Account Wipe Issue
item.content:encoded:
Battlestate Games has made players aware of an issue in Escape from Tarkov that’s plaguing PvE players. As of this morning, seemingly every PvE player has had their accounts wiped or had their progress mixed up with their PvP account, but Battlestate is on the case.

In a post on social media, the team outlined the issue and insisted it was working to restore progress as soon as possible, which isn’t too difficult to do, but it has the community up in arms and actively lambasting the studio.

UPDATE: This issue has now been resolved by Battlestate Games. Original report continues below…

PvE Account Wipe Problem Hits Tarkov

This isn’t the first time that a progress glitch has hit Escape from Tarkov, and it probably won’t be the last time. What matters is that Battlestate Games is resilient enough to recover effectively, but there will undoubtedly be a historic point from which the studio can recover progress, and the most recent raids players were on will have been lost.

That’s conjecture based on prior incidents, I should say.

In the post on social media, the team wrote:

We are aware that, due to a technical issue, progress on players’ accounts in the PvE ZONE has been reset, or profile progress in PvP mode has been transferred to the PvE ZONE.

We are actively working to restore progress for all accounts affected by this issue.

We apologise for any inconvenience caused.

Of course, players were quick to jump into the comments to demand compensation in the form of a promo code, free items, or instant progress boosts (like free levels), but it’s not clear if and when Battlestate will provide anything. I’m holding out the hope that they do, as I too am a ‘PvE main’ player.

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you were struck by the Tarkov PvE wipe issue.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that a new God of War game has been revealed

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP
